title: 7 Things to Know About Payment Solutions for Iranian Digital Sellers Before You Start
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: As a digital seller in Iran, you're all too familiar with the frustration of not being able to use PayPal or Stripe to receive payments. These popular payment processors have been blocked in Iran, forcing you to seek out alternative solutions just to get paid for your work. But with so many options out there, it's hard to know where to start. You've probably spent countless hours researching, trying to find the perfect payment solution that fits your needs. And yet, you still haven't found one that truly works for you. Maybe it's time to consider a different approach - one that uses blockchain technology to process payments and bypass the need for traditional payment processors altogether. In this article, we'll explore the key things you need to know about payment solutions for Iranian digital sellers, so you can finally start getting paid for your work.
The Full List With Context

To be successful, you need to understand the full range of payment solutions available to you. This includes everything from traditional payment processors like Payoneer and Skrill, to newer blockchain-based solutions like NOWPayments and Web3 Wallet Connect. Each of these options has its own strengths and weaknesses, and some may be more suitable to your needs than others. For example, NOWPayments offers support for over 300 different cryptocurrencies, making it a great option for sellers who want to accept a variety of payment methods. On the other hand, Web3 Wallet Connect ($19.99) offers a user-friendly interface and live price feeds, making it a great option for sellers who want to stay on top of market trends. When evaluating these options, it's essential to consider factors like fees, security, and user experience to find the best fit for your business.
Why Each Item on This List Matters

When it comes to payment solutions, the devil is in the details. For instance, some payment processors may charge high fees for each transaction, eating into your profit margins. Others may have strict requirements for KYC (Know Your Customer) verification, which can be a major headache for sellers who operate in jurisdictions with strict regulations. Even worse, some payment processors may be scams or phishing operations, designed to steal your sensitive information. To avoid these pitfalls, it's crucial to do your research and evaluate each payment solution carefully before making a decision.
The Ones Most Creators Get Wrong

One of the biggest mistakes creators make when it comes to payment solutions is trying to use a single solution for all their transactions. This can lead to oversimplification, where a single solution is used for all transactions, without considering the nuances of each transaction. For example, a creator may use a single payment processor for all their transactions, without considering the differences in fees, security, and user experience for each transaction. By trying to use a single solution for all transactions, creators risk alienating their customers, losing revenue, and undermining their business. Instead, creators should focus on using a variety of payment solutions to maximize their revenue and minimize their losses.
The blockchain has no map of restricted countries — a wallet in Lagos processes identically to one in London.
How to Prioritise What to Implement First

When it comes to implementing payment solutions, it's essential to prioritize what needs to be done first. This may involve starting with the most popular payment methods, such as credit card payments or bank transfers. Alternatively, you may want to prioritize the use of blockchain-based solutions, which can offer greater security and faster transactions. Whatever your priority, it's crucial to focus on the most important aspects first, such as security, fees, and user experience. By prioritizing these key areas, you can ensure that your payment solution is robust, reliable, and user-friendly.
Putting the List Into Action

Now that you have a better understanding of the key things you need to know about payment solutions for Iranian digital sellers, it's time to put this knowledge into action. Start by evaluating each payment solution carefully, considering factors like fees, security, and user experience. Next, prioritize what needs to be done first, focusing on the most important aspects such as security, fees, and user experience. Finally, implement your chosen payment solution and test it thoroughly to ensure it works as expected. By following these steps, you can finally start getting paid for your work and take your business to the next level.

Your Action Checklist

Confirm which coins have the best P2P conversion options in your country before you configure your payment settings — USDT on Tron is a safe starting point for most African and South Asian markets. Create your NOWPayments account and add wallet addresses for your primary coins. Deploy the Core Shop Engine with your product files and NOWPayments credentials. Set up Web3 Wallet Connect so buyers can see live prices and QR codes for payment. Run a complete test transaction on mainnet and confirm delivery works end to end. Set up a P2P exchange account — Binance P2P, a local exchange, or both — so you have a conversion route ready the moment your first real sale comes in. Publish your store and share the URL with your existing audience. Track your first transactions in the Admin Dashboard and confirm the conversion rate you are getting from P2P exchange.
Geographic neutrality is not a feature of crypto payments — it is the fundamental property of how blockchains work.
